relative_path: Documents - permits, forms, datasheets, handbooks etc./BSL2_documents_Färkkilä_lab/BSL1 and 2/Action Plan in case of an emergency _Färkkilä_Vähärautio_Cell Culture.docx
full_text:
Last updated 10.9.2020, Anastasiya Chernenko

Action plan in case of an emergency situation in cell culture room

In case cell culture rooms B324a1 ja B324a2 located in Biomedicum Helsinki building need to be urgently evacuated, for example, because of the water damage, all liquid cell and tissue cultures located in this rooms are either discarded by adding 2% Virkon Disinfectant-solution in water or, circumstances allowing, frozen and moved to deep freezer storage. Solid cell culture waste is discarded according to the Waste handling instructions - situation allowing, brought to P-floor’s cold waste storage to wait for a pick-up, if that room is not flooded. Cell and tissue culture volumes used are quite small, so major spread to the environment is not possible and thus is not a threat. In addition, cells are unlikely to survive in the conditions different from optimal used in cell culture incubator (temperature, pH, sterility).

In case of fire cell cultures do not cause risk, as they will be easily destroyed by the high temperatures, neither they are able to form aerosols.

If cell culture HEPA-filtered laminar or cell culture CO2-incubator gets broken, work is stopped and the cultures are either destroyed by adding 2% Virkon Disinfectant-solution in water or moved to the other laminar or incubator or frozen and moved to the deep freezer for storage.

In case cells containing solution splashes onto one’s eye-, mouth- or nose mucous membranes,

the contaminated area is rinsed with running tap water for at least 10 minutes (EYE SHOWER is located in room B314, Medical Genetics, Right after Sampsa Hautaniemi office, behind ONCOSYS grey door). After that contact Occupational Heathcare doctor (MehiläinenYmpyrätalo, tel. 010 414 00).

Gloves and lab coat (optionally also face mask and goggles) are worn when working with cell lines or processing patient-derived samples. In case cell culture or patient material splashes contaminate skin, disinfect the affected skin area with 70% ethanol and then thoroughly wash with soap under running tap water.

Stub and cut wounds are prevented by using protective gear (gloves, lab coats) as well as correct and safe working technique and appropriate tools.

If cell culture solution or patient material contaminates wounded skin (for example, if spills penetrate the wet lab coat) or under the skin through the stab – wash the affected area with 70% ethanol and after it with running tap water and soap, dry the skin. After that contact Occupational Healthcare doctor (MehiläinenYmpyrätalo, tel. 010 414 00).

If biological substances originating from cell culture or patient material contaminate the centrifuge or cell culture room surfaces, spills are washed away with 1% Virkon Disinfectant-solution in water and then with 70% ethanol. If spills contaminate clothes, the stains are disinfected with 70% ethanol and/or trashed together with solid cell culture waste.

In case Virkon solution and its powder meant for disinfection of the liquid wastes and spills unexpectedly finishes it is borrowed from the neighboring laboratory and then ordered via City-lab laboratory supplies company.

In case -80oC and -150oC (B320 and B304b, correspondingly) deep freezers where cell lines and patient-derived samples are stored are malfunctioning all the stored materials are moved to the neighboring lab freezers or to 5th floor (-80oC ) and P-floor (-150oC) spare freezers. Deep freezers are connected to the centralized alarm system. In case of an alarm outside of the office hours the responsible persons (names and phone numbers are listed on the freezers’ doors as well as below) are contacted by a service worker in charge who first receives the alarm. The responsible person comes to the laboratory and moves the materials from the broken freezer to a spare freezer where they will stay until the malfunctioning equipment is fixed.

First aid cabinet, which includes among others bandages to treat small wounds, is located on the hallway wall next to the room B317a.

Occupational Healthcare contact details are: MehiläinenYmpyrätalo, tel. 010 414 00.

Introductory safety training responsible persons are laboratory managers of each laboratory working in the cell culture room:

Färkkilä and Vähärautio lab – Anastasiya Chernenko

Kauppi lab – Minna Tuominen and Taina Turunen

Tang lab – Johanna Eriksson

Responsible persons contact details:

Anastasiya Chernenko (044-0526706), Färkkilä and Vähärautio lab

Ashwini Nagaraj (040-3275249), Färkkilä lab

Jun Dai (044-9883938), Vähärautio lab

Johanna Eriksson (050-5011206), Tang lab
